title: Nizam QA Verdict Schema
description: Validates .agent/qa/NNN.json, the evaluator's pass/fail verdict for a feature, recording each verification command's exit code and an evidence file path per DD-3.

feature_id.description: Id of the feature this verdict evaluates.

verdict.description: The overall QA outcome for this feature.

executed_at.description: Date or date-time the QA checks were executed.

checks.description: One entry per contract verification command that was executed.

evidence.description: Path to the externalised evidence file capturing this check's terminal output.

adversarial_check.description: The evaluator's own additional adversarial spot-check, beyond the contract's listed verification commands.

required_fixes.description: Fixes the generator must make before this feature can pass QA. Empty when verdict is 'pass'.

feature_id.description: Id of the feature this verdict evaluates.

qa_pass.description: The overall QA outcome for this feature.

checks_run.description: Number of contract verification commands independently re-run by the evaluator.

checks_passed.description: Number of those commands that passed (exit 0).

adversarial.description: The evaluator's mandatory adversarial spot-check, beyond the contract's listed verification commands.

performed.description: Whether the adversarial spot-check was actually performed.

evidence_files.description: Paths to the externalised evidence files capturing each check's terminal output.

issues.description: Defects found during QA, beyond required_fixes.

unsupported_claims.description: Claims made by the implementation or contract that QA could not substantiate.

missing_acceptance_coverage.description: Acceptance criteria not actually covered by any executed verification command.

required_fixes.description: Fixes the generator must make before this feature can pass QA. Empty when qa_pass is true.

approved.description: Whether this feature's implementation is approved.

summary.description: Human-readable summary of the QA round's findings and disposition.
